questions.question: What's the difference between verification and validation?
questions.answer:
Core Difference:

  • Verification → “Are we building the product right?” - Checks if the product is built correctly as per requirements.
  • Validation → “Are we building the right product?” - Checks if the product meets user needs.

🧪 Examples

🔹 Verification Example

  • Reviewing requirement documents
  • Checking design documents
  • Code reviews

👉 No code execution happens here.

🔹 Validation Example

  • Testing login functionality
  • Checking if the app behaves correctly for users

👉 Actual execution of the application happens.

questions.question: How do you design test cases for mobile apps on iOS/Android?
questions.answer:
1. Start with Understanding the App

Before writing test cases:

  • Identify core features (login, payments, navigation, etc.)
  • Understand user flows (first-time user vs returning user)
  • Check platform guidelines:
    • Android (Material Design)
    • iOS (Human Interface Guidelines)

🧩 2. Break Features into Test Scenarios

Example: Login Feature

  • Valid login
  • Invalid credentials
  • Empty fields
  • Social login (Google/Apple)
  • Session timeout

Then convert each scenario into detailed test cases.

📱 3. Consider Mobile-Specific Factors

This is where mobile testing stands out:

🔹 Device Fragmentation

  • Different screen sizes, resolutions
  • OS versions (Android 10, 11, 12… / iOS versions)
  • Manufacturers (Samsung, OnePlus, etc.)

👉 Your test cases should mention device/OS coverage

🔹 Network Conditions

  • 2G / 3G / 4G / Wi-Fi
  • No network / intermittent network

👉 Example:

  • Verify app behavior when network drops during API call

🔹 Interruptions

  • Incoming calls
  • SMS
  • Notifications
  • App going to background

👉 Example:

  • Verify app resumes correctly after a call interruption

🔹 Battery & Performance

  • Low battery behavior
  • App launch time
  • Memory usage

🔹 Permissions

  • Camera, location, storage, microphone

👉 Example:

  • Deny location permission → verify fallback behavior

🔄 4. Platform-Specific Test Cases

Even for same feature, behavior differs:

  • Back button (Android) vs gestures (iOS)
  • Push notifications handling
  • UI differences

👉 Always create separate test cases for Android & iOS where needed

🧪 5. Types of Test Cases to Include

  • Functional testing
  • UI/UX testing
  • Usability testing
  • Compatibility testing
  • Security testing
  • Performance testing

📝 6. Write Structured Test Cases

A good test case includes:

  • Test Case ID
  • Title
  • Preconditions
  • Steps
  • Expected Result
  • Actual Result (during execution)
  • Status
